title: Love, Again
scripture_text: Love bears all things, believes all things, hopes all things.
reflection: Friendship is a living thing. It dies from neglect faster than from anything else. Send the text. (The third hearing of this day.)
body:
You have been meaning to call them. For weeks. For months. The message has been drafted in your head a dozen times.

The friendship is not dead. But it is sleeping, and the longer it sleeps, the harder it is to wake.

Send the text. Not a long one. "Thinking of you. Hope you are well." That is enough. That is the whole assignment.

Friendship is a thousand small acts of remembering. The remembering is the love. The remembering is what makes the friend a friend.

You have been loved in small, ordinary ways. The lunch packed. The blanket pulled up. The look across the room that meant, I see you. The friend who did not say anything, but stayed. The coworker who covered for you. The stranger who returned the wallet. The grandparent who remembered your name.

These are the love of God, distributed through the human. They are not random. They are not luck. They are the Lord, who is love, loving you in the only language the moment would allow.

You do not have to wait for a big love. The big love is the small love, repeated. The Lord is the one who stayed. The Lord is the one who came. The Lord is the one who keeps coming.

The love you have been given, you have also been given to give. Look for one person, today, to give it to.

You have been loved in small, ordinary ways. The lunch packed. The blanket pulled up. The look across the room that meant, I see you. The friend who did not say anything, but stayed. The coworker who covered for you. The stranger who returned the wallet. The grandparent who remembered your name.

These are the love of God, distributed through the human. They are not random. They are not luck. They are the Lord, who is love, loving you in the only language the moment would allow.

You do not have to wait for a big love. The big love is the small love, repeated. The Lord is the one who stayed. The Lord is the one who came. The Lord is the one who keeps coming.

There is a way of being in the world that does not require you to be ahead, or impressive, or on top of it. The world will not validate this way. The world will tell you to hurry. The Lord is telling you to be still. The Lord is telling you to be here. The Lord is the one who sees you, right now, in this chair, with this breath, with this much strength, with this much doubt. He sees you. He is with you. The seeing is the practice. The being-with is the gift.

The love you have been given, you have also been given to give. Look for one person, today, to give it to.

This is the third time the year has come around to this day. You have been here before. The Lord has been here before. The body remembers. The soul remembers. The grace is the same.
prayer: Lord, thank You for friends. Help me to be a better one. Show me one I should reach out to today. Amen.
